revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大歳神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[山口県]][[下関市]]竹崎町一丁目13-10 |位置 = |祭神 = [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫神]]（大歳御祖神） |社格 = |創建 = [[文治]]年（[[1186年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }}
'''大歳神社'''（おおとしじんじゃ）は[[山口県]][[下関市]][[竹崎町]]にある[[神社]]。
== 由緒 == [[平安時代]]末期の[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]において、[[平氏|平家]]追討のため西国へ下った[[源義経]]が、武運の守護神・富士[[浅間神社]]の御祭神・大歳御祖大神に戦勝祈願を行った。義経は平家が布陣する[[彦島]]を望む有明山（大歳山・円山）に富士の小松を植え、[[篝火]]を焚き、二日二夜の[[沐浴]]して潔斎を行い、祈念を込めた[[クワ|桑]]の弓矢をもって[[平知盛]]の率いる平家軍に開戦の矢文を放った。
平家軍は急遽[[壇ノ浦]]に軍船を進めて戦ったが、待機していた[[源範頼]]の軍勢と義経軍の挟み撃ちにあい、平家は敗れ、義経率いる[[源氏]]軍の勝利となった。翌年の[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、四軒の漁民が義経の祈願のありさまを畏敬して[[神祠]]を祀ったことが大歳神社の由来となる。
以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。
== アクセス == *[[下関駅]]から徒歩5分
== 関連項目 == *[[壇ノ浦の戦い]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおとししんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[category:下関市]] [[Category:山口県の観光地]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 由緒 == [[平安時代]]末期の[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]において、[[平氏|平家]]追討のため西国へ下った[[源義経]]が、武運の守護神・富士[[浅間神社]]の御祭神・大歳御祖大神に戦勝祈願を行った。義経は平家が布陣する[[彦島]]を望む有明山（大歳山・円山）に富士の小松を植え、[[篝火]]を焚き、二日二夜の[[沐浴]]して潔斎を行い、祈念を込めた[[クワ|桑]]の弓矢をもって[[平知盛]]の率いる平家軍に開戦の矢文を放った。

以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。

== 由緒 == [[平安時代]]末期の[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]において、[[平氏|平家]]追討のため西国へ下った[[源義経]]が、武運の守護神・富士[[浅間神社]]の御祭神・大歳御祖大神に戦勝祈願を行った。義経は平家が布陣する[[彦島]]を望む有明山（大歳山・円山）に富士の小松を植え、[[篝火]]を焚き、二日二夜の[[沐浴]]して潔斎を行い、祈念を込めた[[クワ|桑]]の弓矢をもって[[平知盛]]の率いる平家軍に開戦の矢文を放った。
平家軍は急遽[[壇ノ浦]]に軍船を進めて戦ったが、待機していた[[源範頼]]の軍勢と義経軍の挟み撃ちにあい、平家は敗れ、義経率いる[[源氏]]軍の勝利となった。翌年の[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、四軒の漁民が義経の祈願のありさまを畏敬して[[神祠]]を祀ったことが大歳神社の由来となる。
以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。
== 画像 == <gallery> ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg </gallery> == アクセス == *[[下関駅]]から徒歩5分
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[[壇ノ浦の戦い]]

平家軍は急遽[[壇ノ浦]]に軍船を進めて戦ったが、待機していた[[源範頼]]の軍勢と義経軍の挟み撃ちにあい、平家は敗れ、義経率いる[[源氏]]軍の勝利となった。翌年の[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、四軒の漁民が義経の祈願のありさまを畏敬して[[神祠]]を祀ったことが大歳神社の由来となる。
以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。

{{神社 |名称= 大歳神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|大歳神社拝殿]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |所在地= [[山口県]][[下関市]]竹崎町一丁目13-10 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫神]]（大歳御祖神） |社格= 旧村社 |創建= [[文治]]年（[[1186年]]） |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大歳神社'''（おおとしじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[下関市]][[竹崎町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[平安時代]]末期の[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]において、[[平氏|平家]]追討のため西国へ下った[[源義経]]が、武運の守護神・富士[[浅間神社]]の御祭神・大歳御祖大神に戦勝祈願を行った。義経は平家が布陣する[[彦島]]を望む有明山（大歳山・円山）に富士の小松を植え、[[篝火]]を焚き、二日二夜の[[沐浴]]して潔斎を行い、祈念を込めた[[クワ|桑]]の弓矢をもって[[平知盛]]の率いる平家軍に開戦の矢文を放った。 平家軍は急遽[[壇ノ浦]]に軍船を進めて戦ったが、待機していた[[源範頼]]の軍勢と義経軍の挟み撃ちにあい、平家は敗れ、義経率いる[[源氏]]軍の勝利となった。翌年の[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、四軒の漁民が義経の祈願のありさまを畏敬して[[神祠]]を祀ったことが大歳神社の由来となる。 以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。 == 画像 == ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == アクセス == *[[下関駅]]から徒歩5分 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[[壇ノ浦の戦い]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとししんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:下関市の建築物]] [[Category:下関市の歴史]] [[Category:山口県の旧村社]] [[Category:治承・寿永の乱]] [[Category:関門海峡]]

revision.text:
大歳神社_(下関市)
[[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]]

[[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Ōtoshi Shrine |Image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |Coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == Images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

[[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]][[category:new pages]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Ōtoshi Shrine |Image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |Coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == Images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:大歳神社]]

Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == Images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == Images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |Coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Founded = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == Images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

[[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:大歳神社]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代type制度}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (qq86) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In qq86 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (qq86) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In qq86 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (qq86) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In qq86 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]][[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}[[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い}} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱|12=draft|12=draft}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft|12=draft}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛|12=draft|12=draft}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=draft|12=draft}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼|12=draft|12=draft}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊|12=draft|12=draft}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=draft|12=draft}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=draft|12=draft}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅|12=draft|12=draft}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]

The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}}

revision.comment: 19 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大歳神社 (下関市) up to rev 94050195
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大歳神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|大歳神社拝殿]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |所在地= [[山口県]][[下関市]]竹崎町一丁目13-10 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲耶姫神]]（大歳御祖神） |社格= 旧村社 |創建= [[文治]]年（[[1186年]]） |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''大歳神社'''（おおとしじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[下関市]][[竹崎町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 由緒 == [[平安時代]]末期の[[治承・寿永の乱|源平合戦]]において、[[平氏|平家]]追討のため西国へ下った[[源義経]]が、武運の守護神・富士[[浅間神社]]の御祭神・大歳御祖大神に戦勝祈願を行った。義経は平家が布陣する[[彦島]]を望む有明山（大歳山・円山）に富士の小松を植え、[[篝火]]を焚き、二日二夜の[[沐浴]]して潔斎を行い、祈念を込めた[[クワ|桑]]の弓矢をもって[[平知盛]]の率いる平家軍に開戦の矢文を放った。 平家軍は急遽[[壇ノ浦]]に軍船を進めて戦ったが、待機していた[[源範頼]]の軍勢と義経軍の挟み撃ちにあい、平家は敗れ、義経率いる[[源氏]]軍の勝利となった。翌年の[[文治]]2年（[[1186年]]）、四軒の漁民が義経の祈願のありさまを畏敬して[[神祠]]を祀ったことが大歳神社の由来となる。 以来、武運長久の神として[[明治維新]]の際には[[奇兵隊]]旗揚げの[[軍旗]]が奉納された。大[[鳥居]]は[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に[[白石正一郎]]が[[攘夷]]必勝を祈念して奉納した。 == 画像 == ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg ファイル:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == アクセス == *[[下関駅]]から徒歩5分 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} *[[壇ノ浦の戦い]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとししんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:下関市の建築物]] [[Category:下関市の歴史]] [[Category:山口県の旧村社]] [[Category:治承・寿永の乱]] [[Category:関門海峡]]

Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}} {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)}} {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]
