{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|下関市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuya-hime|qq=}} (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture|qq=}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern typeing System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern typeing System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == History == During the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genpei War|qq=}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|ja|平氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira clan|qq=}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|浅間神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|ja|平知盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Tomomori|qq=}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|ja|彦島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshima|qq=}}. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|ja|源範頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Noriyori|qq=}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|ja|奇兵隊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiheitai|qq=}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|Shimonoseki Station|ja|下関駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonoseki Station|qq=}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg|250px|Ōtoshi Shrine Worship Hall]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuya-hime]] (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]]. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]]. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from [[:en:Shimonoseki Station|Shimonoseki Station]] == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]]

Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{coord|33|57|5.2|N|130|55|31.8|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuya-hime]] (Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami) |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]]. Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]]. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from [[:en:Shimonoseki Station|Shimonoseki Station]] == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:山口県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}}

Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]]. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine. Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論}}. == images == File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg == Access == * 5 minutes on foot from [[:en:Shimonoseki Station|Shimonoseki Station]] == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}} * [[:en:Battle of Dan-no-ura|Battle of Dan-no-ura]] {{translated page|ja|大歳神社 (下関市)|version=94050195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Genpei War]] [[Category:Kanmon Straits]] }} [[sn:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] [[ja:大歳神社]] [[ja:Ōtoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimonoseki]] [[Category:Son-sha in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11436757]] Wikidata name: Ōtoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Takezakichō|qid=Q11599126}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|qq=}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== History ==
During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11436757]] Wikidata name: Ōtoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Takezakichō|qid=Q11599126}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|qq=|1=Takezakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599126}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== History ==
During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|qq=|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|qq=|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11436757]] Wikidata name: Ōtoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Takezakichō|WD=Q11599126}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|1=Takezakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599126}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== History ==
During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11436757]] Wikidata name: Ōtoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Takezakichō|WD=Q11599126}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|1=Takezakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599126}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== History ==
During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11436757]] Wikidata name: Ōtoshi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = [[:en:Shimonoseki|Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Takezakichō|WD=Q11599126}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|1=Takezakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599126}}, [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]].
== History ==
During the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] in the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]], on his campaign to defeat the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira clan]], prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's [[:en:Sengen shrine|Asama Shrine]].
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the [[:en:Kiheitai|Kiheitai]] militia donated their battle flag. The large [[:en:Torii|Torii]] was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.
== images == <gallery> File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya5.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya3.jpg File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya2.jpg </gallery>
== Access == * 5 minutes on foot from [[:en:Shimonoseki Station|Shimonoseki Station]]

Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the [[:en:Taira no Tomomori|Taira no Tomomori]]-led Taira fleet positioned at [[:en:Hikoshima|Hikoshima]].
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of [[:en:Minamoto no Noriyori|Minamoto no Noriyori]]. The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|en|Minamoto no Noriyori|WD=Q3142238|lt=Minamoto no Noriyori}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|en|Kiheitai|WD=Q710429|lt=Kiheitai}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|en|Kiheitai|WD=Q710429|lt=Kiheitai}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== History ==
During the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira clan}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine}}.
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Mount Ariake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|en|Taira no Tomomori|WD=Q736706|lt=Taira no Tomomori}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|en|Hikoshima|WD=Q11288918|lt=Hikoshima}}.
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|en|Minamoto no Noriyori|WD=Q3142238|lt=Minamoto no Noriyori}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|en|Kiheitai|WD=Q710429|lt=Kiheitai}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Otoshi-jinja (Shimonoseki)]][[ja:大歳神社 (下関市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ōtoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimonoseki Ootoshijinjya.jpg |location = {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|WD=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki City, Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Shimonoseki}}, Takezaki-chō 1-chōme 13-10 |coordinates = {{Coord|33.95144444444445|N|130.9255|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |consecration_year = Bunji Era (1186) |Main Hall = |Alias = |Related Sites = |Festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Takezakichō|WD=Q11599126|1=Takezakichō}} }} '''Ōtoshi Shrine''' (大歳神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|ja|竹崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takezaki-chō, Shimonoseki|1=Takezakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11599126}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It holds the status of a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== History ==
During the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}} in the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}, on his campaign to defeat the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira clan|1=Taira clan}}, prayed for victory to Ōtoshi-no-Mioya no Kami, the guardian deity of martial fortune, enshrined at Mount Fuji's {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|WD=Q3471785|lt=Asama Shrine|1=Sengen shrine}}.
Yoshitsune planted a pine tree from Mount Fuji on {{ill|Mount Ariake|ja|有明山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ariake|1=Q11516432|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516432}} (also known as Ōtoshi-yama or Enzan), lit signal fires, bathed in the sea for purification, and prepared for battle. Using a bow made from mulberry wood, he fired a ceremonial opening arrow at the {{ill|Taira no Tomomori|en|Taira no Tomomori|WD=Q736706|lt=Taira no Tomomori|1=Taira no Tomomori}}-led Taira fleet positioned at {{ill|Hikoshima|en|Hikoshima|WD=Q11288918|lt=Hikoshima|1=Hikoshima}}.
The Taira fleet moved to {{ill|Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}} but fell into a pincer attack by Yoshitsune's forces and those of {{ill|Minamoto no Noriyori|en|Minamoto no Noriyori|WD=Q3142238|lt=Minamoto no Noriyori|1=Minamoto no Noriyori}}. The Genji forces emerged victorious. In 1186 (Bunji 2), four local fishermen enshrined the deity to commemorate Yoshitsune's prayers, marking the origin of Ōtoshi Shrine.
Since then, the shrine has been revered as a deity of martial fortune. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the {{ill|Kiheitai|en|Kiheitai|WD=Q710429|lt=Kiheitai|1=Kiheitai}} militia donated their battle flag. The large {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} was donated in 1862 (Bunkyū 2) by {{ill|Shiraishi Shōichirō|ja|白石正一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraishi Shōichirō|1=Shōichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580193}} as a prayer for victory in the {{ill|Jōi Movement|ja|攘夷運動|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōi Movement|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 攘夷論|1=jōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498795}}.
